message:
fix(fp-frame): Series.unique retains a single null marker (frankenpandas-378p)

pandas Series([3, 1, NaN, 3, NaN, 2]).unique() returns
[3, 1, NaN, 2] — NaN counts as a distinct category and is kept in
its first-seen position with subsequent NaN occurrences deduped.
The previous implementation unconditionally dropped every missing
value, silently stripping a semantically meaningful category from
the result.

Introduces a `missing_seen` latch so the first missing value is
retained (regardless of NullKind variant) and subsequent ones are
skipped. First-seen ordering for non-missing values is preserved
unchanged via the existing semantic_eq scan.

Test migrated from "…_skipping_nulls" shape to "…_retaining_one_null"
to reflect pandas parity; new test covers the all-null corner case
where the NaN marker still appears exactly once.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
